commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the stork, the heron after its kind, and the hoopoe, and the bat. Heron, or "stork "noted for the same quality: chasida, means "piety. " Charadrion, a kind of heron, (Calmet) mentioned by Aristotle, viii. It is found in deep holes and rocks. (Menochius) Some translate parrot, peacock, kite Anapha,may denote a bird easily vexed. (Calmet) Houp, or lapwing. (Haydock) Bat. Strabo (xvii. ) speaks of some very large, which were salted and eaten at Borsippe.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
